title: Philip Morris: Marketing Is Working, Strong Growth From Alternatives Makes It A Good Buy
text: Philip Morris International is focusing on alternatives to traditional cigarettes to stay relevant and tap into the growing market for smoke-free products. The company's efforts in cigarette alternatives I think are paying off, with a projected $20 billion in revenue from this segment by 2025. Despite potential health risks and controversies, Philip Morris remains financially strong and offers a potential upside for long-term investors.

title: 5 ETFs to Make the Most of Philip Morris's Q2 Earnings Beat

title: Philip Morris Offers Value Following Earnings Beat
text: Philip Morris International Inc. ( PM , Financial) released a stellar second-quarter earnings report on Thursday, which led to a surge in its stock price. The world's largest tobacco company beat its revenue target by $290 million and trumped its earnings per share estimate by 12 cents, suggesting that market analysts underappreciated its resilience against a shaky economy.

title: Philip Morris boosts revenue guidance as tailwinds rise
text: Philip Morris (NYSE: PM), the giant tobacco company, published strong Q2 results that beat analysts estimates. The company's revenue jumped by 14.9% to $9 billion, about $290 million higher than estimates.

title: Compared to Estimates, Philip Morris (PM) Q2 Earnings: A Look at Key Metrics

title: Philip Morris (PM) Beats Q2 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: Philip Morris (PM) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.60 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.48 per share. This compares to earnings of $1.48 per share a year ago.

title: Philip Morris says smoke-free alternatives ignite Q2 performance
text: Philip Morris International Inc (NYSE:PM) has reported second-quarter earnings that beat expectations, boosted by strong sales of smoke-free alternatives including Swedish Match.  The Marlboro owner raised its stake in Swedish Match, which produces wet snuff snus products and tobacco-free nicotine ZYN pouches, to 93% last year, in a move away from cigarettes.

title: Philip Morris Earnings Outlook Falls Short
text: The maker of Marlboro cigarettes, Philip Morris, posted better-than-expected earnings for the second quarter.

title: Philip Morris stock edged up after profit beats expectations but full-year outlook is a bit shy
text: Shares of Philip Morris International Inc. PM, +0.43% edged up 0.4% in premarket trading, after the cigarette seller topped second-quarter profit expectations, but provided a somewhat downbeat full-year outlook as industry volumes are expected to decline. Net earnings per share fell to $1.01 from $1.43, while EPS excluding nonrecurring items, increased to $1.60 from $1.48 to beat the FactSet consensus of $1.50.

title: Philip Morris: Assessing The Impact Of ZYN
text: Philip Morris, a leading cigarette manufacturer, is shifting its focus towards a "smoke-free future" and diversifying its product portfolio beyond tobacco and nicotine. PM has found success in the smokeless and tobacco-free nicotine market with its product ZYN, which holds a 67.3% market share. Despite potential health concerns, management remains optimistic about the future of its non-combustible products, though regulatory and public perception risks remain.

title: Buy Philip Morris For Your Dividend Growth Portfolio
text: Philip Morris International is a solid investment opportunity with a focus on smoke-free products, offering a hefty dividend payment and a fair valuation. The company's primary growth driver is its smoke-free products, with a 25% increase in units sold in Europe last year. Risks include tight regulation of tobacco products, health consciousness reducing the percentage of smokers, and increased competition in the smoke-free product market.

title: Philip Morris International: An Interesting Dividend Play
text: I recommend Philip Morris International Inc. as a good investment for dividend investors due to its strong dividend yield and robust financial performance. The company is diversifying its product portfolio with smoke-free products like IQOS and Nicocig, and has a diversified geographic exposure with significant revenue from emerging markets. Despite facing challenges such as regulatory uncertainty, litigation risks, and competition, the company has opportunities for growth in the smoke-free product segment and new markets.

title: Philip Morris: The Growth Story Is Just Beginning
text: Philip Morris International is seeing growth driven by its smoke-free products IQOS and ZYN. PM expects a strong Q2 and reaffirmed its full-year forecast. The company plans to reintroduce IQOS in the U.S. market next year, which could be a strong growth driver.
